fragility_points: No clean official FIDE live standings page was captured in this run.
Contextual sources may share underlying information and are not fully independent.
key_assumptions: Secondary live standings accurately reflect the event state.
No fair-play, appeal, withdrawal, or scoring dispute materially changes the field.
A two-point lead with two rounds left is overwhelming but not equivalent to official settlement.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves on an actual FIDE-declared winner, not merely on a dominant in-progress position.
Secondary sources indicate Sindarov is in a hugely favorable late-stage position.
Because two rounds and possible tiebreaks still remain, residual competitive and administrative tails are not zero.
Therefore the right view is strongly yes, but slightly below the 95.05% market price.
main_thesis: Sindarov is very likely to win, but the market is slightly overconfident because the tournament is not yet officially settled and residual tiebreak/admin tails still exist.
quote_anchors: two points clear with two rounds to go
source_quality_view: Good enough for a high-confidence directional yes, but not perfect for a 95%+ certainty claim because live-state verification relied mainly on secondary reporting.
strongest_disconfirmers: A two-point lead with two rounds left in this format may make 95% entirely fair or even slightly low if standings are accurate.
The bearish case is mostly about tail-risk pricing rather than a plausible mainline loss narrative.
strongest_supports: Contextual standings show Sindarov leading 9/12 by two points with two rounds left.
Chess.com round-12 coverage says he is guaranteed at least a playoff.
WSJ context supports that his run is genuinely dominant, not cosmetic.
timing_relevance: Very high; this is a late-stage tournament market where each remaining round can move the probability sharply.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact official live standings/tiebreak math were not reconstructed from a primary FIDE event page.
Low-probability off-board complications remain hard to rule out completely.
what_would_change_view: An official FIDE confirmation that Sindarov has clinched or clean official standings with no complications would push the estimate up; contradictory official reporting or a sharply narrowed lead would push it down.
